text: 1. **TimeoutException class** — Defined at `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/errors/TimeoutException.java:22` as `public class TimeoutException extends RetriableException`. 2. **Record batch expiration logic** — Two key files: (a) `RecordAccumulator.java` defines `expiredBatches(long now)` at line 465 which iterates over queued batches and collects those past the delivery timeout; (b) `Sender.java` calls `this.accumulator.expiredBatches(now)` at line 407 and constructs the exact error message `"Expiring N record(s) for topic-partition"` at line 416, then fails the batch with a `TimeoutException`. `Sender.java` also has its own `expiredInflightBatches` logic (line 181) for in-flight requests. 3. **`delivery.timeout.ms` configuration** — Defined in `ProducerConfig.java` at line 165 as `public static final String DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = "delivery.timeout.ms"`, with a default of 120000ms (line 398). The dependency chain flows: ProducerConfig defines the timeout → RecordAccumulator checks batch age against it → Sender triggers expiration and throws TimeoutException.
